scenes.narration: My dad asked a great question: can a model really make a video from a text command? Yes, but the important part is how. This project gives the model a structured way to describe visuals instead of guessing every pixel.

children.content: Can AI make video from text?
Yes. But give it a visual language.

scenes.narration: Today, state of the world demos often ask multimodal models to generate video directly. That can look impressive, but across frames the layout drifts, objects jump, and the story can lose consistency.

That happens because language models are good at symbols, plans, and instructions. Video asks for spatial relationships, timing, continuity, and visual judgment. Pixels are not really the model's native language.
So instead of saying: paint the video, we say: write a plan in the Kaivra language. The plan defines what exists, where it goes, how it moves, and what the narrator says.
From there, the architecture becomes straightforward. A prompt goes to the LLM. The LLM writes Kaivra JSON. Then a schema validator checks that the structure makes sense before we render anything.
Once the plan is valid, the engine builds a scene graph, computes layout, schedules motion on a timeline, and renders to video or the web. One source can drive multiple outputs.

This also gives us consistency. The same object ids can persist across scenes, layout stays semantic, and continuity rules make motion feel coherent. It starts to behave like animation software, not a sequence of unrelated slides.
Because it is software, an agent can work on it step by step. It can validate, render a sample, inspect frames, patch the JSON, add voiceover, and rerender. That is agentic development.

This matters because people live in a visual world, not a text-only world. We use diagrams, timing, voice, screens, and feedback. The next step is software that lets agents plan, test, inspect, and refine visual work.

children.content: next step: software, not bigger prompts

scenes.narration: So the value is not just that an LLM can make a video. The value is that it can collaborate inside a system built for clarity, consistency, iteration, and human communication. That is what Kaivra is for.
